title: The regex escape sequence \cX should only be used with characters in the @-_ range
description: The regex escape sequence \cX is only valid when X is a character in the range @ to _. Using it with other characters can lead to undefined or unintended behavior in regular expressions. This rule detects and flags such invalid usage.
recommendation: Use the \cX escape sequence only with characters in the @-_ range. For other control characters, use the appropriate escape or Unicode notation.
impact: Incorrect use of \cX can cause regular expressions to behave unexpectedly, potentially leading to bugs or security issues.

noncompliant:
Pattern.compile("\cA"); // Compliant
Pattern.compile("\cZ"); // Compliant
Pattern.compile("\c!"); // Noncompliant, ! is not in @-_ range
Pattern.compile("\c9"); // Noncompliant, 9 is not in @-_ range

checks.message: Use the \cX escape sequence only with characters in the @-_ range. For other control characters, use the appropriate escape or Unicode notation.
